title: Financial Military Benefits
content:
Military Educational Benefits
Morgan State University

---

OVERVIEW

Morgan State University proudly embraces its "military-friendly" status by actively recruiting and supporting Veterans. We are committed to meeting the academic and social needs of Veteran students and enhancing their experience on campus.

---

FUTURE INITIATIVES

Morgan State University is working on:
- Establishing an on-campus Veteran Club
- Fostering an environment sensitive to Veterans' needs
- Promoting available Veterans' resources
- Creating a centralized on-campus work-study office for Veterans

---

TYPES OF MILITARY BENEFITS

GI Bill Benefits:
- Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33)
- Montgomery GI Bill (Chapter 30)
- Montgomery GI Bill Selected Reserve (Chapter 1606)
- Survivors' and Dependents' Educational Assistance (Chapter 35)

Other Benefits:
- Yellow Ribbon Program participation
- Tuition Assistance
- Vocational Rehabilitation (Chapter 31)

---

HOW TO USE YOUR BENEFITS

1. Apply for benefits through the VA (va.gov)
2. Obtain your Certificate of Eligibility (COE)
3. Submit COE to Morgan State's Veterans Services
4. Complete enrollment certification each semester
5. Maintain satisfactory academic progress

---

CONTACT INFORMATION

Veterans Engagement Services Coordinator and Certifying Official:
Shirleene Prioleau

Phone: 443-885-3042
Email: veterans@morgan.edu

---

ADDITIONAL RESOURCES

VA Education Benefits Hotline: 1-888-442-4551
VA Website: www.va.gov/education/

Morgan State Veterans Services can assist with:
- Enrollment certification
- Benefits questions
- Academic advising for Veterans
- Connecting with campus resources
- Navigating the transition to college life
